title: Where Poetry Actually Finds You — Even When You're Not Looking
description:
I'm a data scientist. I also write poetry. For years I treated those as separate identities — until I noticed the same curiosity was driving both.

This video is about where poetry actually shows up: in your code, in burnt toast on a Monday morning, in the grief that insists on specificity long after you've accepted the facts.

Poetry doesn't stay in its lane. It never did. The question is whether you notice when it arrives.

[TIMESTAMPS_PLACEHOLDER]

If a line has stayed with you — something you read years ago that still lives in your chest — leave it in the comments. I read them all.

[LINKS_PLACEHOLDER]

Subscribe for weekly videos on data science, self-development, and the things that live between them.
thumbnail_concept: Split composition: left half shows a dark terminal screen with green code glow; right half shows handwritten poem lines on warm cream paper in soft window light. Center text overlay: 'Poetry Was Already There' in clean white serif. Mood — cold technical blue meets warm literary amber, divided cleanly down the middle.
end_screen_suggestion: Surface the nearest Life & Self-Development video on identity or dual-life themes; alternatively a poetry reading playlist from the channel.
